input.new_string:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k p10k prompt style
# Shows: user@host  dir  git-branch  model  context%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name')
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')

# Shorten home directory to ~
home="$HOME"
short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$home|~|")

# Git branch (skip optional locks to avoid blocking)
git_branch=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null \
           || git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
    git_branch=" \033[0;33m $branch\033[0m"
  fi
fi

# Context percentage
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining")
  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[0;31m"   # red when low
  elif [ "$remaining_int" -le 40 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[0;33m"   # yellow when medium
  else
    ctx_color="\033[0;32m"   # green when plenty
  fi
  ctx_part=" ${ctx_color}ctx:${remaining_int}%\033[0m"
fi

printf "\033[0;36m$(whoami)@$(hostname -s)\033[0m  \033[0;34m${short_cwd}\033[0m${git_branch}  \033[0;35m${model}\033[0m${ctx_part}"

content.content: The file /home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully.
The file /home/nick/.claude/settings.json has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Here is a summary of what was configured:

**Script:** `/home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh`

**Settings updated:** `/home/nick/.claude/settings.json`

**What the status line shows** (inspired by your p10k left prompt segments):

- `user@host` in cyan
- current directory (with `~` for home) in blue
- git branch with a  icon in yellow (when inside a git repo)
- Claude model name in magenta
- context remaining percentage in green/yellow/red depending on how much is left

Since your p10k prompt does not use a traditional `PS1`, the status line was designed from scratch to mirror the key p10k elements: directory and git status are the core of your left prompt, so those are the focus here.

---

Note: for any future changes to this status line, please use the **statusline-setup** agent (the same agent type that just ran). You can ask Claude Code at any time to "update my status line to show X" and it will invoke this agent to make the changes.

Here is a summary of what was configured:

**Script:** `/home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh`

**Settings updated:** `/home/nick/.claude/settings.json`

**What the status line shows** (inspired by your p10k left prompt segments):

- `user@host` in cyan
- current directory (with `~` for home) in blue
- git branch with a  icon in yellow (when inside a git repo)
- Claude model name in magenta
- context remaining percentage in green/yellow/red depending on how much is left

Since your p10k prompt does not use a traditional `PS1`, the status line was designed from scratch to mirror the key p10k elements: directory and git status are the core of your left prompt, so those are the focus here.

---

Note: for any future changes to this status line, please use the **statusline-setup** agent (the same agent type that just ran). You can ask Claude Code at any time to "update my status line to show X" and it will invoke this agent to make the changes.

Done! Your status line is now configured to mirror your p10k prompt style, showing:
- **user@host** (cyan)
- **current directory** (blue)  
- **git branch** (yellow) when in a repo
- **Claude model** (magenta)
- **context remaining** (green/yellow/red)

The configuration is stored in `/home/nick/.claude/settings.json` with a helper script at `/home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh`. You can update it anytime by asking Claude Code to adjust the status line display.

message.content: do I have to restart session to make it effective?

content.text: No, you shouldn't need to restart. The status line should become effective immediately on your next Claude Code interaction or command. If it doesn't appear right away, you can try closing and reopening your session, but it's usually not necessary.

lastPrompt: do I have to restart session to make it effective?
